questions.question: How many elements on a page can share the same id attribute value?
questions.explanation: The id attribute must be unique within a page. Only one element should have a given id value. For shared identifiers, use the class attribute instead.

questions.question: How do you assign multiple classes to a single HTML element?
choices: Separate class names with spaces inside one class attribute
Wrap each class name in quotes
questions.explanation: Multiple classes are assigned by separating class names with spaces within a single class attribute, e.g., class="btn primary large".

questions.question: Which CSS selector targets an element with id="header"?
questions.explanation: In CSS, the hash symbol (#) is used to select elements by their id. The dot (.) is used for class selectors.

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid class name in HTML?
questions.explanation: Class names should not start with a digit, cannot contain spaces (spaces separate multiple classes), and should not include the dot selector. 'my-class' follows proper naming conventions.

questions.question: In CSS, a class selector is prefixed with a ___ symbol.
questions.explanation: CSS class selectors use a dot (.) prefix, e.g., .my-class { color: red; }. This distinguishes them from id selectors which use #.

questions.question: The ___ attribute must have a unique value within an HTML document.
questions.explanation: The id attribute must be unique per page. Unlike classes, which can be reused across many elements, each id value should appear only once.
